title: Pseudo-fumaria minor
intro: Pseudo-fumaria minor, also known as False Fumitory, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves, and white or yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudo-fumaria minor include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Pseudo-fumaria minor is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Pseudo-fumaria minor is a species of plant in the family Papaveraceae. It is a small, annual or biennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant is often found in dry, sandy soils and rocky outcrops. Pseudo-fumaria minor is a member of the genus Pseudo-fumaria, which is part of the larger order Ranunculales. This classification places Pseudo-fumaria minor within the broader context of flowering plants.
